title: Force weapon
content:
{{Top|leg}}
[[File:StickVsSaber-TOTJDLOTS1.jpg|thumb|right|250px|[[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] uses a Force-imbued [[Master Vodo's quarterstaff|walking stick]] to duel [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]]]]
{{Quote|Through the Force I can make my simple staff more powerful than ''any'' lightsaber.|Vodo-Siosk Baas|<ref name="DarkLordsaudio">[[Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith (audio drama)|''Tales of the Jedi: Dark Lords of the Sith'' audio drama]]</ref>}}
'''Force Weapon''' was a [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Force power/Legends|ability]] that allowed the user to imbue an unpowered weapon with the Force for a period of time. By channeling energy into the weapon, it allowed the wielder to strike and do more damage than the weapon's simple appearance would suggest. As a useful side effect, a Force-imbued weapon could be used to block [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] without damaging the weapon.<ref name="DarkLords1">[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 1]]</ref>

The ancient [[Je'daii]] of [[Tython/Legends|Tython]],<ref name="FaD">[[Star Wars: Force and Destiny Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Core Rulebook]]</ref> and later the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], Force-imbued [[Force-imbued sword/Legends|their swords]]<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> using [[Vur Tepe]].<ref name="NoP">''[[Nexus of Power]]''</ref>

Most of the users of this power were those outside the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], many from cultures and Force traditions that were considered quite primitive, such as the [[Zeison Sha]], who used this power to augment their [[discblade]]s,<ref name="HG">''[[Hero's Guide]]''</ref> though there were some exceptions. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Vodo-Siosk Baas]] used this power in his duels against [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], using his humble [[Master Vodo's quarterstaff|walking stick]] to counter Kun's lightsaber skills.<ref name="DarkLords1" /><ref name="SithWar3">[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 3]]</ref>

The [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Jungle Felucian/Legends|natives]] of [[Felucia/Legends|Felucia]] were also known to employ such an ability, infusing their [[Felucian Skullblade|jawbone swords]] with the Force to increase their cutting power and allow them to block lightsabers.<ref name="TFU">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''</ref>

[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and [[Galen Marek|Starkiller]] were able to imbue and charge their lightsabers with the Force. In Starkiller's case, his lightsaber emitted [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] or [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force Push]], granting him attack capabilities that opponents were rarely able to predict, much less counter.<ref name="TFU" />

Force-imbued weapons could also damage and destroy dark side spirits.<ref name="DSS">''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
It is also possible that [[Kreia]] might have used this power during her adventures with [[Meetra Surik]]. In ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', whenever Kreia uses a two-handed wield there is a purple glow from the severed hand.
